name: Netherlands Antilles (1959-1986)

colors.symbolism: love and the bond with the Netherlands
the Caribbean Sea and the bond with the Netherlands

description: A white field with a horizontal blue band and a vertical red band crossing at the centre. On the red band, six white five-pointed stars arranged in a circle.
history: Adopted on 19 November 1959 as the flag of the Netherlands Antilles, the autonomous Dutch Caribbean territory established by the Charter for the Kingdom of the Netherlands in 1954. The six stars represented the six constituent islands. When Aruba seceded from the federation to become a separate constituent country of the Kingdom of the Netherlands on 1 January 1986, the flag was modified to remove one star, yielding the five-star version that flew until the Netherlands Antilles was itself dissolved on 10 October 2010.

features.symbolism: a horizontal blue band crossed by a vertical red band on a white field — combining the colours of the Netherlands flag in a distinctive arrangement

features.role: Six stars on the central red band
features.symbolism: the six island territories of the Netherlands Antilles: Aruba, Bonaire, Curaçao, Saba, Sint Eustatius, and Sint Maarten
